name: Anna Kowalczyk
education: MSc in Computer Science – Warsaw University of Technology
summary: Anna is a highly experienced backend engineer with over nine years of experience delivering scalable and secure enterprise systems within the banking and financial sector. She has led multiple initiatives involving microservices transformation, event-driven architecture, and cloud migration. Known for her strong problem-solving abilities and pragmatic approach, she actively contributes to system design and architectural decisions. She has also mentored junior developers and collaborated closely with product owners and business stakeholders to translate requirements into robust technical solutions.

projects.name: Core Banking System Modernization
projects.description: Led development of microservices replacing legacy monolithic banking core. Designed event-driven communication using Kafka and implemented scalable APIs used across multiple business units. Played a key role in system performance optimization and migration to AWS cloud infrastructure.
projects.technologies: Java, Spring Boot, Kafka, PostgreSQL, AWS

projects.name: Payment Gateway Integration Platform
projects.description: Built highly available integration layer connecting multiple payment providers and banking APIs. Focused on transaction security, high throughput processing, and resilience. Collaborated with cross-functional teams to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.

name: Piotr Nowak
education: BSc in Computer Science – Poznań University of Technology
summary: Piotr is a frontend developer with 4 years of experience building modern, responsive web applications with a strong focus on usability and performance. He has worked on large-scale eCommerce platforms handling high traffic and complex user journeys. He is comfortable collaborating closely with designers and backend developers to deliver intuitive and efficient solutions. Piotr consistently works on improving frontend performance metrics and implementing best practices in state management and component design.

projects.name: Large-scale eCommerce Platform
projects.description: Developed and optimized product listing and checkout flows in a high-traffic eCommerce platform. Improved page load times and SEO performance using Next.js and code-splitting. Worked closely with UX team to enhance customer journey and conversion rates.

projects.name: B2B Marketplace
projects.description: Implemented dynamic dashboards and order management views for vendors. Focused on usability, accessibility compliance, and responsiveness across devices. Contributed to reusable component library and improved maintainability of the codebase.

name: Tomasz Zieliński
education: MSc in Computer Science – AGH University of Science and Technology
summary: Tomasz is a seasoned DevOps engineer with over six years of experience designing and maintaining cloud-native infrastructure. He specializes in automation and reliability, enabling teams to deliver software faster and more safely. He has extensive experience working with AWS environments, Kubernetes clusters, and implementing CI/CD pipelines. Tomasz actively drives improvements in system observability, incident response processes, and infrastructure cost control, contributing significantly to operational excellence.

projects.name: Cloud Migration Program
projects.description: Led migration of legacy systems from on-premise to AWS cloud, designing scalable and fault-tolerant infrastructure using Terraform and Kubernetes. Ensured high availability and smooth transition with zero downtime deployments and monitoring improvements.

projects.name: CI/CD Platform Implementation
projects.description: Designed and implemented enterprise-grade CI/CD pipelines across multiple development teams. Reduced deployment times and improved release frequency while ensuring quality and rollback mechanisms. Introduced monitoring and alerting standards.

name: Karolina Mazur
education: MSc in Data Science – University of Warsaw
summary: Karolina is a data scientist with 5 years of experience building predictive models and delivering analytical insights for healthcare and insurance domains. She has strong expertise in machine learning pipelines and translating complex data findings into business value. She collaborates closely with stakeholders to define use cases and validate results, ensuring practical application of models. Her work includes predictive analytics, risk modeling, and optimization of operational processes using data.

projects.name: Disease Prediction Model
projects.description: Developed machine learning models to predict patient risk profiles based on historical medical data. Improved early detection capabilities and supported clinical decision-making. Worked closely with domain experts to validate model assumptions and ensure interpretability.

projects.name: Insurance Risk Scoring System
projects.description: Designed and implemented a scoring engine for evaluating insurance risks. Built data pipelines and feature engineering processes, improving prediction accuracy and supporting underwriting decisions across multiple markets.

name: Michał Kaczmarek
education: MSc in Software Engineering – Wrocław University of Science and Technology
summary: Michał is an experienced .NET developer with over seven years of hands-on experience in designing and implementing enterprise-grade backend systems. He has worked extensively on distributed architectures and integrations with external systems. Michał is known for writing clean, maintainable code and for his ability to bridge technical and business perspectives. He often takes a leading role in projects, supporting teams in making sound architectural decisions and ensuring high-quality delivery.

projects.name: Insurance Claims Management System
projects.description: Developed backend services for processing insurance claims, integrating multiple third-party data providers. Focused on reliability, data consistency, and compliance with business rules. Contributed to architecture design and overall system scalability.

projects.name: Logistics Platform Integration
projects.description: Built integration layer connecting internal logistics systems with external partners. Designed APIs and handled high-volume data exchange, ensuring stability and performance across distributed services.

name: Jakub Wiśniewski
education: BSc in Computer Science – Gdańsk University of Technology
summary: Jakub is a full-stack developer with five years of experience building SaaS products from database schema to polished UI. He works comfortably across NestJS backends and React frontends, with a strong focus on type safety and maintainable module boundaries. He regularly pairs with product managers to slice features into shippable increments.

projects.name: Multi-tenant SaaS Platform
projects.description: Built tenant-aware APIs and admin dashboards for a B2B subscription product. Implemented role-based access control, audit logging, and background job processing for billing workflows.

projects.name: Real-time Collaboration Module
projects.description: Delivered live document editing features using WebSockets and optimistic UI updates. Improved perceived performance and reduced conflict resolution issues for concurrent users.

name: Ewa Dąbrowska
education: MSc in Computer Science – Jagiellonian University
summary: Ewa is a solution architect with eleven years of experience designing large-scale systems for regulated industries. She translates business capabilities into target architectures, migration roadmaps, and integration standards. She is trusted in RFP processes and leads architecture review boards for delivery teams.

projects.name: Target Architecture for Digital Bank
projects.description: Defined microservices boundaries, event catalog, and API standards for a greenfield digital banking program. Coordinated migration from legacy middleware to cloud-native messaging.

projects.name: Insurance Policy Administration Modernization
projects.description: Created reference architecture for policy lifecycle services and external partner integrations. Reduced coupling between core systems and improved release independence for product teams.

name: Marcin Lewandowski
education: BSc in Information Technology – Łódź University of Technology
summary: Marcin is a QA automation engineer with six years of experience raising quality in fast-moving product teams. He designs reliable end-to-end suites, integrates tests into CI pipelines, and coaches developers on testability. He balances automation depth with pragmatic manual exploration for edge cases.

projects.name: E2E Automation for Streaming Platform
projects.description: Introduced Playwright-based regression suite covering subscription flows, content playback, and payment edge cases. Cut release verification time by half while improving defect detection before production.

projects.name: API Contract Testing Initiative
projects.description: Implemented contract tests between mobile, web, and backend services. Reduced integration defects during parallel team delivery and standardized API change notifications.

name: Agnieszka Wójcik
education: MSc in Mobile Computing – Warsaw University of Technology
summary: Agnieszka builds production mobile applications for FinTech and mobility clients using React Native. She focuses on smooth navigation, secure credential storage, and resilient offline behavior. She has shipped multiple apps to App Store and Google Play with staged rollout practices.

projects.name: Mobile Banking App Refresh
projects.description: Rebuilt customer-facing banking app with improved biometric login, transaction notifications, and accessibility support. Optimized startup time and reduced crash rate across older Android devices.

projects.name: Fleet Driver Application
projects.description: Developed driver workflow app with GPS tracking, digital proof of delivery, and offline sync. Integrated with backend dispatch APIs and push notification services.

name: Łukasz Kamiński
education: MSc in Cybersecurity – Military University of Technology
summary: Łukasz is a security engineer with eight years of experience embedding security into software delivery. He runs threat modeling sessions, hardens cloud configurations, and partners with developers to fix vulnerabilities early. He has led responses to production security incidents and improved detection coverage.

projects.name: OAuth2 Platform Hardening
projects.description: Reviewed and redesigned authentication flows for a multi-product SaaS platform. Introduced token rotation, scope minimization, and centralized session revocation.

projects.name: Cloud Security Baseline Program
projects.description: Defined secure defaults for AWS accounts, CI/CD secrets handling, and container image scanning. Automated compliance checks in deployment pipelines across twelve product teams.

name: Natalia Zając
education: BSc in Computer Science – Silesian University of Technology
summary: Natalia builds internal developer platforms that help teams deploy services safely and quickly. She creates golden-path templates, self-service environments, and observability defaults. Her work reduces operational toil and standardizes how dozens of services run in production.

projects.name: Internal Developer Portal
projects.description: Launched Backstage-based portal with service catalog, scaffolding templates, and ownership metadata. Accelerated onboarding for new engineers and improved visibility of system dependencies.

projects.name: GitOps Deployment Standard
projects.description: Rolled out GitOps workflows with Argo CD for fifty microservices. Standardized promotion paths from dev to production and integrated automated smoke tests after sync.

name: Katarzyna Szymańska
education: MSc in Computer Science – University of Wrocław
summary: Katarzyna designs and operates data pipelines that feed analytics, reporting, and machine learning use cases. She emphasizes reliable orchestration, tested transformations, and clear data contracts between producers and consumers. She has migrated legacy ETL jobs to modern cloud-native stacks.

projects.name: Retail Sales Data Platform
projects.description: Built nightly and near-real-time pipelines ingesting POS, eCommerce, and inventory feeds into a curated warehouse layer. Enabled unified customer analytics across online and store channels.

projects.name: Streaming Events Pipeline
projects.description: Implemented Kafka-to-warehouse streaming for clickstream and campaign events. Added data quality checks and alerting for schema drift and late-arriving records.

name: Bartosz Król
education: BSc in Computer Science – Nicolaus Copernicus University
summary: Bartosz specializes in low-latency backend services written in Go. He builds resilient APIs and workers that handle high request volumes with clear metrics and graceful degradation. He is comfortable profiling production bottlenecks and simplifying over-engineered modules.

projects.name: Real-time Bidding Service
projects.description: Developed sub-50ms bidding engine processing millions of requests per hour. Optimized memory allocation, connection pooling, and cache strategies for peak traffic events.

projects.name: Shipment Tracking API
projects.description: Created gRPC-based tracking service integrating carrier webhooks and internal warehouse systems. Improved traceability and reduced support tickets for delayed deliveries.

name: Magdalena Piotrowska
education: MSc in Applied Computer Science – AGH University of Science and Technology
summary: Magdalena builds Python backends for products that require dependable workflows and clear domain boundaries. She uses FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, and Celery to deliver features with strong test coverage. She enjoys refactoring legacy Django monoliths into maintainable service modules.

projects.name: Patient Portal Backend
projects.description: Implemented appointment booking, lab results delivery, and secure messaging APIs for a healthcare provider. Ensured GDPR-compliant audit trails and role-based data access.

projects.name: Online Learning Platform API
projects.description: Built course enrollment, progress tracking, and payment webhook handlers. Introduced background jobs for certificate generation and email notifications.

name: Rafał Jankowski
certificates: Microsoft Certified: Azure Solutions Architect Expert
education: BSc in Telecommunications – Poznań University of Technology
summary: Rafał implements and operates Azure environments for enterprise clients migrating from on-premise data centers. He designs hub-spoke networks, hybrid connectivity, and automated provisioning pipelines. He tracks cloud spend and recommends right-sizing without compromising reliability.

projects.name: Azure Landing Zone Rollout
projects.description: Deployed standardized subscriptions, policies, and networking for a manufacturing group. Enabled self-service AKS clusters with guardrails and centralized logging.

projects.name: Hybrid Identity Integration
projects.description: Integrated on-premise Active Directory with Entra ID for single sign-on across SaaS and custom applications. Reduced password-related support load and improved access reviews.

name: Joanna Grabowska
education: MSc in Software Engineering – Warsaw University of Technology
summary: Joanna leads a squad of eight engineers delivering payment and billing capabilities for a FinTech scale-up. She balances hands-on code reviews with roadmap planning, unblocking teams and maintaining engineering standards. She has grown two mid-level developers into tech leads.

projects.name: Subscription Billing Engine
projects.description: Led team building recurring billing, dunning, and invoice generation services. Coordinated cross-team API contracts and phased rollout to enterprise merchants.

projects.name: Merchant Dashboard Rewrite
projects.description: Directed frontend modernization from legacy jQuery to React with shared design system. Improved merchant self-service and reduced support escalations.

name: Damian Kowalski
education: BSc in Computer Science – University of Gdańsk
summary: Damian improves production reliability for high-traffic SaaS products. He defines SLOs, builds alerting that pages humans only when necessary, and automates remediation for common failure modes. He has reduced mean time to recovery through runbooks and game days.

projects.name: SLO Framework Implementation
projects.description: Introduced SLI/SLO dashboards and error budget policies for core customer journeys. Aligned product and engineering priorities based on reliability trade-offs.

projects.name: Auto-remediation for Kubernetes
projects.description: Built controllers and scripts to restart unhealthy pods, scale nodes, and drain noisy neighbors. Decreased overnight pages and improved availability during traffic spikes.

name: Aleksandra Nowicka
certificates: Certified Professional in Accessibility Core Competencies
education: BSc in Computer Science – SWPS University
summary: Aleksandra crafts complex React applications with emphasis on reusable components and accessible interactions. She maintains design system libraries and guides teams on performance budgets and testing strategies. Her UIs consistently score well on Core Web Vitals.

projects.name: Design System for HR SaaS
projects.description: Authored component library with Storybook documentation, theming, and accessibility tests. Accelerated feature delivery across four frontend squads.

projects.name: Property Management Dashboard
projects.description: Built data-dense dashboards with virtualization and optimistic updates for lease workflows. Improved task completion time for property managers.

name: Szymon Walczak
education: MSc in Information Systems – University of Economics in Katowice
summary: Szymon designs integration layers connecting core banking, ERP, and partner systems. He selects appropriate patterns—sync APIs, messaging, or file exchange—and documents canonical data models. He has untangled brittle point-to-point integrations into manageable platforms.

projects.name: Banking Integration Hub
projects.description: Architected central integration platform for account, payment, and CRM systems. Standardized error handling, retries, and correlation IDs across fifty interfaces.

projects.name: Utility Meter Data Exchange
projects.description: Built reliable ingestion pipeline for smart meter readings from multiple vendors. Enabled near-real-time billing adjustments and anomaly detection.

name: Monika Cieślak
education: MSc in Artificial Intelligence – University of Warsaw
summary: Monika bridges data science and production engineering by building pipelines that train, deploy, and monitor machine learning models reliably. She automates retraining triggers and drift detection while keeping models auditable for compliance teams.

projects.name: Recommendation Model Platform
projects.description: Deployed batch and online inference paths for product recommendation models. Integrated A/B testing hooks and performance dashboards for data scientists.

projects.name: Manufacturing Defect Detection Pipeline
projects.description: Operationalized computer vision models on edge gateways with centralized monitoring. Reduced false positives and improved feedback loops for model retraining.

name: Filip Górski
education: BSc in Computer Science – Wrocław University of Science and Technology
summary: Filip writes high-performance systems components in Rust for security and networking products. He focuses on predictable latency, safe concurrency, and thorough benchmarking before production rollout. He also maintains FFI boundaries with legacy C++ modules.

projects.name: Packet Inspection Engine
projects.description: Rewrote critical path of network inspection tool from C++ to Rust. Achieved lower memory usage and eliminated several classes of concurrency bugs.

projects.name: Game Server Matchmaking Core
projects.description: Built matchmaking service handling thousands of concurrent sessions. Optimized queue algorithms and region-aware routing for competitive latency targets.

name: Weronika Jastrzębska
education: BSc in Computer Science – Lublin University of Technology
summary: Weronika develops native Android applications with modern Kotlin and Jetpack Compose. She implements clean architecture patterns, robust offline modes, and analytics-friendly navigation. She has maintained apps with millions of downloads through structured release trains.

projects.name: Travel Booking Android App
projects.description: Implemented search, booking, and itinerary features with offline cache for travelers. Integrated payment SDKs and deep links from marketing campaigns.

projects.name: Food Delivery Courier App
projects.description: Built courier workflow with route optimization hooks and proof-of-delivery capture. Improved GPS accuracy handling and battery usage on mid-range devices.

name: Adrianna Kubiak
education: MSc in Computer Science – Poznań University of Technology
summary: Adrianna owns PostgreSQL clusters supporting mission-critical applications. She tunes slow queries, designs indexing strategies, and implements HA topologies with automated failover. She partners with developers to prevent schema changes that compromise performance.

projects.name: PostgreSQL HA Migration
projects.description: Migrated single-node production databases to Patroni-managed clusters with read replicas. Achieved zero-downtime cutover and improved read scalability for reporting workloads.

projects.name: Query Performance Remediation
projects.description: Identified and fixed top twenty slow queries causing checkout timeouts. Introduced monitoring dashboards and review checklist for new database changes.

name: Hubert Malinowski
education: BSc in Telecommunications – Warsaw University of Technology
summary: Hubert designs and maintains enterprise networks for multi-site organizations. He implements secure segmentation, resilient WAN connectivity, and monitored firewall policies. He resolves complex outages involving routing, DNS, and hybrid cloud connectivity.

projects.name: Multi-site WAN Redesign
projects.description: Replaced legacy MPLS with SD-WAN across twelve manufacturing plants. Improved application performance for ERP and VoIP while simplifying branch deployments.

projects.name: Healthcare Network Segmentation
projects.description: Segmented clinical and administrative networks to meet security requirements. Implemented zero-trust access for medical devices and remote clinicians.

name: Oliwia Rutkowska
education: BSc in Computer Science – University of Lodz
summary: Oliwia ships product features end-to-end, from hypothesis and prototype to production metrics review. She combines Next.js full-stack skills with strong product sense, running experiments and iterating based on user behavior. She works closely with design and growth teams.

projects.name: Creator Monetization Features
projects.description: Launched tipping and subscription modules for a creator platform. Built checkout flows, webhooks, and creator dashboards with near-real-time earnings updates.

projects.name: Onboarding Experiment Program
projects.description: Ran A/B tests on signup flows and activation checklists. Increased week-one retention through guided setup and personalized content recommendations.
